examples: Streptokinase and urokinase are important in thrombolytics, and asparaginase is used as a cytostatic agent in the treatment of leukemia.
The availability of new cytostatic agents and other drugs may improve the resectability of liver metastases and prolong the survival in advanced disease.
Objective response is a weak surrogate of activity in phase II trials, since in most cases, the predominant effect of these compounds is basically cytostatic.
